fragmentation_summary: Flood hazard data is authoritative but devolved. England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland publish through separate agencies and delivery stacks. England, Scotland, and Wales expose substantial machine-readable data; Northern Ireland is primarily viewer/PDF based and carries more restrictive reuse conditions.

sources.native_name: Risk of Flooding from Rivers and Sea
sources.english_name: Risk of Flooding from Rivers and Sea

evidence.reason: Direct dataset endpoint selected for bounded validation.

sources.notes: Pass-2 replacement for GB-EA-002. The legacy RoFRS WMS slug (spatialdata/risk-of-flooding-from-rivers-and-sea/wms) returns HTTP 404; the Environment Agency migrated this product to NaFRA2 (Flood Zones produced under NaFRA2 from 25 March 2025). Service now targets the live NaFRA2 rivers-and-sea WMS confirmed by bounded probe (WMS GetCapabilities, 6 layers, EPSG:4326/4258/27700). Primary points at the current canonical catalogue page.

sources.native_name: Risk of Flooding from Surface Water
sources.english_name: Risk of Flooding from Surface Water

evidence.reason: Direct dataset endpoint selected for bounded validation.

sources.notes: Pass-2 replacement for GB-EA-003. The legacy RoFSW WMS slug (spatialdata/risk-of-flooding-from-surface-water/wms) returns HTTP 404; the product is now served under NaFRA2. Service now targets the live NaFRA2 surface-water WMS confirmed by bounded probe (WMS GetCapabilities, 6 layers, EPSG:4326/4258/27700). Primary is the unchanged canonical catalogue page, which remained live.

discovery_metrics.stop_reason: Fixture reached good-enough coverage across all four devolved publishers using the existing catalogue plus focused official-page inspection.
